title: Multi-Instance XMRig Exporter Support
description:
Enable xmrig_exporter to monitor multiple xmrig/xmrig-proxy instances from a single process, exposing all metrics on one Prometheus endpoint with xmrig_target labels to distinguish instances.

Key Design:
- Single MultiCollector using shared dict[str, MetricFamily] pattern
- Dual code path: --url mode (original collectors, no labels) vs --config mode (MultiCollector with xmrig_target labels)
- True backward compatibility: single-target --url mode produces identical output
- YAML config for multi-instance setups
- Label: xmrig_target (not instance, avoiding Prometheus reserved label)

Plan: .omc/plans/multi-instance-support.md

comments.author: fb
comments.text:
Epic complete! All 6 child issues implemented and closed:
✓ US-001: Extract metric-building logic
✓ US-002: Create MultiCollector class  
✓ US-003: Add YAML config support
✓ US-004: Add resilience and _up gauges
✓ US-005: Comprehensive test suite
✓ US-006: Documentation and version bump

Multi-instance support fully implemented with backward compatibility maintained.

title: Step 1: Extract metric-building logic into standalone functions
description:
Refactor XmrigCollector and XmrigProxyCollector to extract JSON-to-metrics logic into standalone functions.

Details:
- Create extract_miner_metrics(json_data, families, extra_labels) function
- Create extract_proxy_metrics(json_data, families, extra_labels) function
- Functions mutate shared dict[str, MetricFamily] (lookup-or-create then append pattern)
- Create add_metric_to_families() helper for the shared mutation logic
- Refactor existing collectors to delegate to these functions
- When extra_labels is None, no extra labels added (backward compatible)

Files: xmrig_exporter/collector.py, xmrig_exporter/proxy_collector.py

Why first: MultiCollector (Step 2) depends on these functions

comments.author: fb
comments.text: Starting implementation - refactoring collectors to extract metric-building logic

comments.author: fb
comments.text:
Implementation complete:
- Added add_metric_to_families() helper with shared dict pattern
- Created extract_miner_metrics() in collector.py
- Created extract_proxy_metrics() in proxy_collector.py
- Both collectors now delegate to extracted functions
- Label ordering uses sorted() for consistency
- Backward compatible with extra_labels=None
- All 9 acceptance criteria verified

title: Step 2: Create MultiCollector class
description:
Create MultiCollector that aggregates metrics from multiple xmrig/xmrig-proxy targets.

Details:
- __init__ takes targets list with: name, url, token, mode, timeout
- collect() iterates targets, fetches JSON once per target
- Auto-detect mode from same JSON response (check for 'miners'/'workers' keys)
- Call extract_miner_metrics() or extract_proxy_metrics() with extra_labels={'xmrig_target': name}
- Maintain single dict[str, MetricFamily] per scrape cycle
- Yield each family once after all targets processed
- Add _up gauge (xmrig_up or xmrig_proxy_up) with value 1 on success, 0 on error
- Error isolation: one failed target doesn't poison entire scrape

Files: xmrig_exporter/multi_collector.py (new)

Depends on: Step 1 (needs extracted functions)

- Emits xmrig_up or xmrig_proxy_up gauge per target

- All 10 acceptance criteria verified

title: Step 3: Add YAML config support and CLI validation
description:
Add --config flag for YAML configuration file and update CLI argument handling.

Details:
- Add --config argument for YAML config file path
- --config and --url are mutually exclusive (argparse group)
- Change --url from required=True to required=False
- Add validation: at least one of --url or --config must be provided
- YAML schema: targets list with name, url, token (optional), mode (miner/proxy/auto)
- Config validation with clear error messages
- When --url: use original XmrigCollector/XmrigProxyCollector (no MultiCollector, no xmrig_target label)
- When --config: use MultiCollector with xmrig_target labels
- Add pyyaml dependency to setup.py

Files: xmrig_exporter/exporter.py, setup.py

Depends on: Step 2 (needs MultiCollector)

- Added pyyaml>=3.12 to setup.py dependencies
- All 11 acceptance criteria verified

title: Step 4: Add resilience and _up gauges
description:
Enhance error handling and add health monitoring gauges.

Details:
- Per-target timeout (default 5s, configurable in config)
- Emit xmrig_up or xmrig_proxy_up gauge (1=reachable, 0=unreachable) with xmrig_target label
- On HTTP error/timeout: log warning with target name, emit _up=0, skip remaining metrics
- Try/except around each target fetch so one failure doesn't affect others
- Log format includes target name and error message

Files: xmrig_exporter/multi_collector.py

Note: Overlaps with Step 2 implementation, can be done together

comments.author: fb
comments.text: Reviewing US-004 - resilience features already implemented in MultiCollector (US-002)

comments.author: fb
comments.text:
Verification complete: All resilience features were already implemented in MultiCollector (US-002):
- Per-target timeout configurable (default 5s)
- HTTP errors caught with try/except per target
- Error logging with target name
- _up=0 emitted on error, _up=1 on success
- Continue to next target on error
All 7 acceptance criteria verified

title: Step 5: Comprehensive test suite
description:
Add pytest-based tests with mocked HTTP responses covering all scenarios.

Test cases:
- Single-target regression: --url produces identical output to current version (no xmrig_target label)
- Multi-target metrics: multiple miners emit correct xmrig_target labels
- Mixed miner+proxy: both xmrig_ and xmrig_proxy_ prefixes coexist
- Failure handling: one failed target doesn't break others, _up=0 emitted
- Config parsing: valid YAML loaded correctly, invalid YAML rejected
- Auto-detection: 'miners'/'workers' keys correctly trigger proxy mode
- Metric merging: same metric name from multiple targets produces one MetricFamily with N samples
- Label consistency: all metrics from same target have same xmrig_target value

Files: tests/ directory (new or existing)

Depends on: Steps 1-4 (needs full implementation)

title: Fix label consistency in _up metrics
description:
Critical: Error path in MultiCollector only includes xmrig_target label, but success path includes both xmrig_target and worker_id. This creates inconsistent label schemas that Prometheus will reject.

Fix: Add worker_id='unknown' to error path up_labels at multi_collector.py:108

comments.author: fb
comments.text:
Fixed:
- Added worker_id='unknown' to error path up_labels in multi_collector.py:108
- This ensures consistent label schema between success (worker_id from API) and error (worker_id='unknown') paths
- Added test_up_gauge_label_consistency_mixed_results to verify fix
- Prevents Prometheus from rejecting metrics due to label schema mismatch
